title: Shuttle fiber frontmatter block
description: The shuttle: YAML block written and validated by the shuttle CLI. A fiber is shuttle-managed iff it carries this block; it dispatches iff the felt-native status is 'active'. There is no enabled flag and no review axis — lifecycle is status + tempered, uniform across kinds.

kind.description: Dispatch mode. oneshot: dispatched once, then status:closed at exit (terminus on accept). standing: recurring per schedule; accept re-arms it. kind exists only to make accept kind-aware.

host.description: Machine id (own_host_id) of the daemon that owns dispatch for this fiber. The dispatch predicate is strict: a daemon dispatches a block iff block.host == its own_host_id. There is no nil-wildcard and no 'local' default — an absent/empty host is unowned and ineligible on every daemon. install/repeat stamp this by default; required for an armed install.

project_dir.description: Absolute or tilde-prefixed working directory on the target host where the worker tmux session should start. Required for an armed install (status:active).

agent.description: Agent ID from the agent registry. Defaults to the registry's default agent when absent.

schedule.description: Present when kind=standing. Defines the recurrence.

expr.description: Standard 5-field cron expression (minute hour dom month dow). Example: '0 9 * * 1-5'.

notes.lifecycle: Lifecycle is status + tempered, uniform across kinds. status:open = draft/paused (not dispatched). status:active + no tempered = armed (oneshot dispatches when deps met; standing when cron.next(now) is due). A live tmux session = running (read from tmux, never stored). Worker exit = status:closed + no tempered = awaiting review (this closed state is the don't-re-fire / anti-oscillation gate). accept: oneshot -> tempered:true (terminus); standing -> status:active + closed-at cleared (re-armed). reject -> tempered:false = composted (terminus, both kinds). The sole dispatch gate is status:active.
notes.writer_ownership: Document fields the CLI writes to synced frontmatter: kind, agent, schedule, host, project_dir. The felt-native status field drives dispatch — the CLI mutates it (open=draft, active=armed, closed=terminus/awaiting) through the lifecycle verbs.
notes.schema_tz_migration: Pre-CLI standing roles used schedule.timezone. Migration renames to schedule.tz. The Elixir daemon reads tz after migration.
notes.kind_migration: Pre-CLI standing roles used shuttle.mode instead of shuttle.kind. Migration renames to kind.
notes.interactive_retired: The `interactive` axis is retired. Per-dispatch 'talk to me first' intent rides the From User directive; structural human-gates (2FA, send-in-his-voice) live in the constitution text; resume-from-kanban is the universal way to talk to any worker. A legacy `interactive: true` in a fiber is inert — the daemon ignores it and the next Go rewrite wipes it.
